hero.title: Discover Minnesota
hero.welcome: Welcome Xinyi, Vivian, Anthony, and Taemin to Minnesota
hero.subtitle: From Prince's Paisley Park to the Boundary Waters — discover a state where music, wilderness, and innovation converge.

home.intro_title: Welcome to the North Star State
home.intro_desc: Whether you have a weekend or a week, Minnesota rewards the curious. Stroll where Prince once walked, trace Bob Dylan's northern roots, or stand at the headwaters of the Mississippi. Explore world-class museums, paddle the Boundary Waters, hike the North Shore, or sample a thriving food and craft brewery scene. Your North Star adventure starts here.

events.preview_subtitle: A quick look at major public events coming up soon. Open the full calendar for every month from May through December 2026.
events.page_subtitle: Major visitor-friendly events across Minneapolis, Saint Paul, and the metro area from May through December 2026.
events.title: May-December 2026 Event Calendar
events.subtitle: A curated visitor calendar of confirmed public events across Minneapolis, Saint Paul, and nearby metro stops. Dates can change, so each card links back to an official source.
events.month_filter: Filter Twin Cities events by month
events.source_note: Compiled from official visitor, city, and event-organizer pages on May 20, 2026. December holiday schedules are still being published, so that card is marked as a watchlist item.

destinations.title: Top Destinations
destinations.subtitle: Tailor your 2-3 day itinerary based on your interests. Here are some of the most remarkable places to visit.

sculpture_garden.name: Minneapolis Sculpture Garden
sculpture_garden.description: An iconic Minneapolis landmark featuring the famous Spoonbridge and Cherry sculpture. Located near downtown Minneapolis, this beautiful garden is a must-visit for art lovers.

moa.name: Mall of America
moa.description: The largest shopping mall in the United States. Originally the site of the Metropolitan Stadium, it now features an indoor amusement park, aquarium, and hundreds of stores.

skyway_system.name: Minneapolis Skyway System
skyway_system.description: The world's largest continuous skyway system, spanning 11 miles and connecting over 80 city blocks. These climate-controlled, glass-enclosed pedestrian bridges let you explore downtown Minneapolis in comfort year-round — a truly unique urban experience.

red_wing.name: Red Wing Town & Boot
red_wing.description: Famous for the Red Wing heritage boots that gained fame during WWII. A picturesque town just an hour's drive from the Twin Cities. Highly recommended for vintage fashion lovers and picking up a memorable pair of boots.

lake_city.name: Lake City
lake_city.description: The birthplace of water skiing. Enjoy stunning summer views that feel like being by the ocean. Located a short, scenic drive from Red Wing.

basilica.name: Basilica of Saint Mary
basilica.description: A prominent and beautiful Catholic basilica located in downtown Minneapolis, known for its stunning architecture.

guthrie.name: Guthrie Theater
guthrie.description: A renowned theater with unique architecture. The 'Endless Bridge' extends into mid-air and offers excellent, free public views. The land was donated by the city with the promise of lifetime free access to the public spaces.

endless_bridge.name: Endless Bridge
endless_bridge.description: The Guthrie's dramatic cantilevered public walkway stretches out toward the Mississippi River and is one of the best free viewpoints in Minneapolis.

amber_box.name: Amber Box
amber_box.description: The Guthrie's striking yellow glass lookout space on the upper levels offers a memorable interior view of the river, Stone Arch Bridge, and the Minneapolis skyline.

st_anthony.name: Saint Anthony Falls
st_anthony.description: The 'Mother Falls' of Minneapolis. This natural wonder powered the city's early milling industry, giving rise to iconic brands like General Mills, Pillsbury, and Häagen-Dazs.

stone_arch_bridge.name: Stone Arch Bridge
stone_arch_bridge.description: The defining Minneapolis landmark for skyline and river views. This historic bridge spans the Mississippi beside Saint Anthony Falls and is one of the best places in the city for a walk and photos.

mill_city_museum.name: Mill City Museum
mill_city_museum.description: One of the best places to understand how Minneapolis was built by flour milling. Set inside the ruins of the Washburn A Mill, it combines city history, river views, and one of the most distinctive museum settings in Minnesota.

mia.name: Minneapolis Institute of Art (Mia)
mia.description: A high-quality museum featuring a vast collection of precious artifacts, including extensive Asian art exhibits. General admission is always free.

capitol.name: Minnesota State Capitol
capitol.description: The seat of the Minnesota state government in Saint Paul, featuring a magnificent dome and beautiful marble architecture.

arboretum.name: Minnesota Landscape Arboretum
arboretum.description: The University of Minnesota's expansive botanical garden, offering beautiful trails and diverse plant collections year-round.

como.name: Como Zoo & Conservatory
como.description: A wonderful, family-friendly destination featuring a zoo and a stunning glass-domed conservatory filled with lush tropical plants.

hill_house.name: James J. Hill House
hill_house.description: The historic, massive mansion of the famous railroad tycoon James J. Hill, located on Summit Avenue in Saint Paul.

cathedral.name: Cathedral of Saint Paul
cathedral.description: A breathtaking Catholic cathedral dominating the Saint Paul skyline, known for its classic Renaissance architecture.

rice_park.name: Rice Park
rice_park.description: A charming downtown Saint Paul park framed by Landmark Center, the Ordway, and the Saint Paul Hotel. It is an easy stop for a stroll, photos, and a quick break between nearby cultural landmarks.

minnehaha_falls.name: Minnehaha Falls
minnehaha_falls.description: One of Minneapolis's most iconic natural landmarks, this 53-foot waterfall in Minnehaha Park is a beautiful stop for a walk, photos, and a quick taste of Minnesota scenery right in the city.

wayzata_lake_street.name: Wayzata Lake Street
wayzata_lake_street.description: A walkable lakeside main street lined with shops, restaurants, and marina views. It is one of the easiest and most pleasant ways to experience Lake Minnetonka from shore.

lake_minnetonka.name: Lake Minnetonka
lake_minnetonka.description: A large, scenic lake west of Minneapolis known for boating, waterfront towns, and broad open-water views. It is a strong pick for a relaxed afternoon drive or sunset stop.

brands.title: Iconic Brands & Companies
brands.subtitle: Minnesota is home to numerous Fortune 500 companies and brands that have shaped the world.

spam.name: Spam
spam.description: Invented by Hormel Foods in Austin, MN. It became a military staple during WWII and remains highly popular, especially in Asian markets.

red_wing_shoes.name: Red Wing Shoes
red_wing_shoes.description: Renowned for high-quality work boots and WWII paratrooper boots.

general_mills.name: General Mills
general_mills.description: A global food giant behind brands like Cheerios, Häagen-Dazs, Betty Crocker, and Yoplait.

pillsbury.name: Pillsbury
pillsbury.description: Famous for flour, baked goods, and cookie dough, represented by the iconic 'Pillsbury Doughboy'.

land_o_lakes.name: Land O'Lakes
land_o_lakes.description: One of America's largest dairy cooperatives, known for premium butter and cheese.

dairy_queen.name: Dairy Queen (DQ)
dairy_queen.description: World-famous ice cream and fast-food chain, headquartered in Bloomington, MN.

buffalo_wild_wings.name: Buffalo Wild Wings
buffalo_wild_wings.description: Famous for chicken wings and its sports bar atmosphere, headquartered in Minneapolis.

target.name: Target
target.description: A retail giant and popular discount department store headquartered in Minneapolis.

best_buy.name: Best Buy
best_buy.description: The largest consumer electronics retailer in the US, headquartered in Richfield, MN.

mayo_clinic.name: Mayo Clinic
mayo_clinic.description: A world-renowned medical institution in Rochester, MN, known for advanced research, diagnosis, and treatment.

medtronic.name: Medtronic
medtronic.description: The world's largest medical device company, producing pacemakers and surgical tools.

unitedhealth.name: UnitedHealth Group
unitedhealth.description: One of the world's largest health insurance and healthcare management companies, based in Minnetonka, MN.

cargill.name: Cargill
cargill.description: The largest privately held agricultural company globally, spanning food processing, trade, and finance.

3m.name: 3M
3m.description: A global innovation powerhouse that invented N95 masks, Post-it notes, and Scotch tape.

polaris.name: Polaris
polaris.description: Manufacturer of ATVs, snowmobiles, and watercraft.

toro.name: Toro
toro.description: Makers of premium landscaping, golf course maintenance, and snow removal equipment.

ecolab.name: Ecolab
ecolab.description: A global leader in water, hygiene, and infection prevention solutions.

us_bank.name: U.S. Bank
us_bank.description: The fifth-largest bank in the United States, headquartered in Minneapolis.

ameriprise.name: Ameriprise Financial
ameriprise.description: A prominent wealth management, insurance, and financial services company.

thrivent.name: Thrivent
thrivent.description: A Christian-based financial company providing insurance, investments, and financial planning.

food_drink.title: Food & Drink
food_drink.subtitle: A practical Twin Cities guide to places worth going for meals, beer, and wine. These are 10 recommended picks per category rather than a strict ranking.

admin.hint: Open this page with ?admin=1 to reorder cards. Drag within a category, then copy the JSON if you want to save that order back into the site data.

spoon_and_stable.name: Spoon and Stable
spoon_and_stable.description: A flagship Minneapolis restaurant in a restored stable building, known for polished New American cooking and one of the strongest all-around dinner experiences in the city.

restaurant_alma.name: Restaurant Alma
restaurant_alma.description: One of Minneapolis's long-running fine dining standards, with a refined seasonal menu and a calm, intimate atmosphere.

hai_hai.name: Hai Hai
hai_hai.description: A bright, energetic restaurant serving Southeast Asian-inspired dishes and cocktails in one of Minneapolis's strongest dining neighborhoods.

meritage.name: Meritage
meritage.description: A dependable downtown St. Paul brasserie for oysters, steak frites, and classic French dishes in an elegant but approachable room.

matts_bar.name: Matt's Bar
matts_bar.description: An iconic South Minneapolis stop for the original Jucy Lucy and a no-frills Minnesota burger experience.

colita.name: Colita
colita.description: A stylish South Minneapolis restaurant centered on modern Mexican cooking, mezcal, and a warm, social atmosphere.

aster_cafe.name: Aster Cafe
aster_cafe.description: A strong pick when the goal is as much the setting as the food, with a riverfront patio and one of the best skyline views in the city.

khaluna.name: Khaluna
khaluna.description: A polished Minneapolis restaurant from chef Ann Ahmed, serving Lao-inspired dishes in a space that works well for both dinner out and visitors.

myriel.name: Myriel
myriel.description: A small St. Paul restaurant with a thoughtful seasonal menu, strong execution, and a quieter neighborhood feel than many headline spots.

porzana.name: Porzana
porzana.description: A stylish steakhouse from the Jester Concepts group, centered on live-fire cooking, strong meat dishes, and a lively higher-end dinner atmosphere.

nolos.name: NOLO's Kitchen & Bar
nolos.description: A reliable North Loop option for groups and visitors, with a broad menu, polished room, and a location that fits naturally into a downtown outing.

smack_shack.name: Smack Shack
smack_shack.description: A well-known seafood spot with a strong local following, especially useful when you want a more energetic casual meal in the North Loop.

surly.name: Surly Brewing
surly.description: A flagship Twin Cities brewery with a large destination taproom, beer hall scale, and one of the most recognizable names in Minnesota craft beer.

summit.name: Summit Brewing
summit.description: A St. Paul classic with deep local roots, known for balanced beer styles and a taproom that makes an easy stop on a brewery-focused afternoon.

indeed.name: Indeed Brewing
indeed.description: A Northeast Minneapolis staple with a friendly taproom and a lineup that ranges from approachable staples to seasonal and experimental pours.

fair_state.name: Fair State Brewing Cooperative
fair_state.description: One of the most respected breweries in the metro, especially strong on lagers, hoppy beers, and a more neighborhood-driven atmosphere.

utepils.name: Utepils Brewing
utepils.description: A west Minneapolis brewery known for European-style beers and an outdoor setting that makes it especially good in warmer months.

blackstack.name: BlackStack Brewing
blackstack.description: A St. Paul favorite for hazy IPAs and a spacious taproom that is easy for groups and casual visits.

pryes.name: Pryes Brewing
pryes.description: A riverfront brewery with a strong patio, reliable food program, and a location that pairs well with time along the Mississippi.

falling_knife.name: Falling Knife Brewing
falling_knife.description: A popular Minneapolis brewery with a more energetic taproom feel and a beer list that leans modern and hop-forward.

bauhaus.name: Bauhaus Brew Labs
bauhaus.description: A well-known Northeast brewery with a broad beer lineup and a social taproom that often feels busy in the best way.

modist.name: Modist Brewing
modist.description: A North Loop brewery known for experimentation and a distinctive voice, while still remaining accessible for visitors.

alexis_bailly.name: Alexis Bailly Vineyard & Winery
alexis_bailly.description: Minnesota's oldest winery, located in Hastings, and a good choice when you want a place with real local wine history.

saint_croix.name: Saint Croix Vineyards
saint_croix.location: Stillwater area, Marine on St. Croix
saint_croix.description: A long-running Stillwater-area winery with tasting room access, scenic grounds, and a convenient day-trip feel from the Twin Cities.

schram.name: Schram Vineyards Winery & Brewery
schram.description: A polished Waconia destination that combines vineyard views with a broader food-and-drink outing atmosphere.

sovereign_estate.name: Sovereign Estate
sovereign_estate.description: A scenic Lake Waconia winery that works especially well for a slower afternoon built around wine, views, and the grounds.

winehaven.name: Winehaven Winery
winehaven.description: A Chisago Lakes destination with a larger estate feel and a comfortable setting for people who want wine tasting without rushing.

seven_vines.name: 7 Vines Vineyard
seven_vines.description: A refined Dellwood winery close enough to the metro for an easy outing, with attractive grounds and a more polished tasting experience.

parley_lake.name: Parley Lake Winery
parley_lake.description: A Waconia-area winery that feels more laid-back and seasonal, especially appealing during warmer weather and event weekends.

cannon_river.name: Cannon River Winery
cannon_river.description: A popular Cannon Falls stop that is a little beyond the core metro, but still a practical and well-known wine day trip.

two_silo.name: Two Silo Winery & Vineyard
two_silo.description: A White Bear Lake-area winery with a farmstead feel, making it one of the easier vineyard-style outings from the Twin Cities side.

wild_mountain.name: Wild Mountain Winery
wild_mountain.description: A Stillwater-area winery with event energy, seasonal programming, and enough space to feel more like an excursion than a quick stop.

peking_garden.name: Peking Garden
peking_garden.description: A longstanding St. Paul Chinese restaurant that is especially useful for classic dishes, family-style meals, and a more old-school neighborhood feel.

mandarin_kitchen.name: Mandarin Kitchen
mandarin_kitchen.description: A long-running Bloomington favorite for dim sum and larger group meals, and still one of the safest Chinese recommendations in the metro.

jade_dynasty.name: Jade Dynasty
jade_dynasty.description: A newer Lyn-Lake destination with a broader traditional Chinese menu and one of the more relevant dim sum options inside Minneapolis proper.

legendary_spice.name: Legendary Spice
legendary_spice.description: A strong choice when the goal is Sichuan-style heat, hot pot, and a menu that goes well beyond standard American Chinese dishes.

tea_house.name: Tea House
tea_house.description: A Twin Cities standby with a very large menu, reliable group-friendly dining, and a mix of familiar and more regional Chinese dishes.

master_noodle.name: Master Noodle
master_noodle.description: A practical pick for hand-pulled noodles, dumplings, and soup-based dishes when you want something more focused and casual.

grand_szechuan.name: Grand Szechuan
grand_szechuan.description: A dependable neighborhood option in Bloomington for spicy Sichuan-leaning dishes and a menu built more for repeat local visits than hype.

ju_yuan.name: Ju Yuan
ju_yuan.description: A useful west-metro option with a broad Chinese menu that works well for straightforward family-style meals.

home_taste.name: Home Taste
home_taste.description: A good pick for people looking for a more regional and less generic Chinese menu, especially on the west side of the metro.

hong_kong_noodle.name: Hong Kong Noodle
hong_kong_noodle.description: A practical choice for noodle soups, roast meats, and quick Cantonese-style comfort food without making the meal feel overly formal.

northern_kitchen.name: Northern Kitchen
northern_kitchen.description: A newer Bloomington-area Chinese spot that is useful for regional dishes and gives the south metro another strong option beyond the older standbys.

patisserie_46.name: Patisserie 46
patisserie_46.description: One of the metro's strongest pastry stops, known for polished French baking, laminated pastries, and a consistent neighborhood following.

marc_heu.name: Marc Heu Patisserie Paris
marc_heu.description: A St. Paul favorite for French pastries, desserts, and a more polished bakery experience that still feels approachable.

rustica.name: Rustica Bakery
rustica.description: A respected local bakery with strong breads, pastries, and cafe staples, especially useful when you want a reliable all-around stop.

isles_bun.name: Isles Bun & Coffee
isles_bun.description: A Minneapolis institution for cinnamon rolls and a classic neighborhood cafe feel that remains easy to recommend to visitors.

black_walnut.name: Black Walnut Bakery
black_walnut.description: A modern bakery with a strong pastry program and the kind of quality that makes it stand out even in a competitive local category.

angel_food.name: Angel Food Bakery & Donut Bar
angel_food.description: A lively downtown Minneapolis stop for donuts, sweets, and bakery treats when the goal is something less formal and more fun.

cafe_ceres.name: Cafe Ceres
cafe_ceres.description: A polished Minneapolis cafe with strong coffee, good pastries, and a clean, contemporary setting that works well for casual meetups.

french_meadow.name: French Meadow Bakery & Cafe
french_meadow.description: A long-running local cafe and bakery with broad appeal, especially useful when you need a place that works for mixed groups.

keys_cafe.name: Keys Cafe & Bakery
keys_cafe.description: A Twin Cities classic that leans more comfort cafe than boutique bakery, but still earns its place for baked goods and local familiarity.

patricks_bakery.name: Patrick's Bakery & Cafe
patricks_bakery.description: A dependable French-style bakery and cafe with pastries, desserts, and a reliable suburban option outside the core city center.

itineraries.title: Suggested Itineraries
itineraries.subtitle: Make the most of your trip with these curated plans designed to showcase the very best of Minnesota.

one_day.title: 1-Day: Twin Cities Highlights
one_day.desc: Perfect for a quick trip focusing on core cultural and historical landmarks.

morning.activity: Start at the Minneapolis Sculpture Garden for iconic photos, then visit the nearby Basilica of Saint Mary.

afternoon.activity: Enjoy world-class art at the Minneapolis Institute of Art (Mia), then head to the historic Saint Anthony Falls.

evening.activity: End the day at the Guthrie Theater—take in the view from the Endless Bridge and perhaps catch a show.

three_day.title: 3-Day: The Full Minnesota Experience
three_day.desc: A well-rounded trip balancing city culture, shopping, and natural beauty.

day_1.activity: Explore the Sculpture Garden and Mia. Then head to St. Paul to marvel at the State Capitol, Cathedral of Saint Paul, and James J. Hill House.

day_2.activity: Spend the morning admiring nature at Como Zoo & Conservatory, then dedicate the afternoon and evening to exploring the Mall of America.

day_3.activity: Head south for a scenic drive along the Mississippi River. Visit Red Wing for its heritage boots and charming town, then continue to Lake City for stunning waterside views.
